**Device Provisioning Template

This document provides a template for the standard procedures required for provisioning a new device setup. It covers hardware assembly, hub configuration, adding peripherals (sensors and cameras), and final tablet provisioning.

# Hardware Assembly for Hub

This section details the physical assembly of all necessary components before powering on or configuring any software.

## Component Checklist

Ensure all required components are present and accounted for:

  

|Component|Status|Notes|
|---|---|---|
|Raspberry Pi|||
|Power Supply|||
|SD card|||
|Zigbee dongle|||
|Hub Casing|||
|Z-wave|||
|Internal power cord|||
|Sticky pad & Screw kit (4)|||
|L-shaped connector|||
|Lid screw|||
|Wire holder|||

## Assembly Steps

1. Screw sticky pads to Pi, ensure pads face inwards, remove film and stick pi to the case according to image
    
2. Add each connector (internal power and L-shaped) and pull through the hole in the case 
    
3. Add wire holder and lid screw to secure the internal power cord 
    
4. Attach zigbee antenna, bend it to the right then plug in zigbee and z-wave dongle
    

  

Notes: 

- Sticky pads are strong, once placed you cannot remove them 
    

  

Validation Steps: 

- Match to Example Kit 
    

# Initialize Hub

## Network Configuration

|Setting|Value|Notes|
|---|---|---|
|SSID (for Wi-Fi)|[Network Name]|Provided by Team|
|Network Password|[Password]|Provided by Team|

  
  

---

# Setting Wi-fi SSID/Password for Provisioning

## Steps to set Wi-fi SSID/Password to match the site’s Wi-fi for provisioning and testing:

1. Connect to the AT&T router (usually named "heylo-provisioning") and go to [http://192.168.1.254/](http://192.168.1.254/).
    
2. Click the "Home Network" tab at the top.
    
3. Within the Home Network section, select the "WiFi" tab.
    
4. When prompted, enter the Admin password (Device Access Code). It is currently set to the default 830??@97/9, but this may change — confirm the current password if the default does not work.
    
5. In the "Home SSID" section, set the Network Name (SSID) to the site's SSID.
    
6. Set the Password to the site's password.
    
7. Scroll down and click the "Save" button (located beneath the Guest SSID section).
    

The WiFi SSID/Password will now update. Any devices connected to the old network SSID will no longer connect and will need their WiFi settings updated to connect to the new SSID.

---

## Flash SD card 

Note: If using the Marconi Workstation, start on Step 1 and skip the prerequisites below.

Prerequisites:

- Make sure the heylo-hub repo is available on your computer. If not, clone it from here. (Add link)
    
- Make sure the latest .wic file is downloaded.
    
- If you are using a Windows machine, make sure WSL is installed and enabled, and that you know your WSL user password.
    
- Make sure you have the Raspberry Pi Imager installed: [Raspberry Pi software – Raspberry Pi](https://www.raspberrypi.com/software/)
    

---

Steps

1. Check the Downloads folder and remove any existing heylo.env file before starting.
    
2. Download the latest .wic file and extract it. (It is usually compressed in .bz2 format and requires a third-party extraction tool on Windows.)
    
3. Download the heylo.env file from our platform at [https://app.heylo.tech](https://app.heylo.tech) as a superuser by selecting the three-dot menu for the specific agency you are creating a hub for and clicking "Download .env". (Refer to image below)
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

4. Place the .wic and heylo.env files in the Downloads folder.
    
5. Open Command Prompt and navigate to the hub directory. On the workstation, run: cd C:\Users\heylo\Documents\Heylo\hub Run the script using the following syntax (include the quotation marks and replace the bracketed variables with actual values):
    

- (Ignore if you are at the workstation at Maconi) Standard Windows (will prompt for your WSL user password during the process):
    
- inject-wic.bat "[WIFI SSID]" "[WIFI PASSWORD]" [PATH TO FOLDER WITH WIC/ENV]
    

- Workstation at Marconi (the default path is C:\\Users\\Heylo\\Downloads — run with no path specified):
    

- inject-wic.bat "[WIFI SSID]" "[WIFI PASSWORD]"
    
- Example: C:\Users\heylo\Documents\Heylo\hub> inject-wic.bat "heylo-provisioning" "heylo123" C:\Users\heylo\Downloads
    
- WSL Password: `heylo123`
    

     6. Once the script finishes, verify the following in the output:

- heylo.env injected successfully.
    
- WiFi SSID and password were set successfully.
    

  

7. Open the Raspberry Pi Imager tool.
    
8. Select Raspberry Pi 5 as the device type.
    
9. Scroll down and select "Use Custom" for the OS type. When prompted, select the .wic file the script was just run on.
    
10. When prompted for storage, ensure the SD card is plugged into the computer and select it.
    
11. Click "Write" with all default options and wait for the flashing process to complete. (This typically takes 10–15 minutes.)
    
12. Done! Remove the SD card and insert it into a hub.
    

---

Validation

Camera note

1. Insert the SD card and plug in the power cord.
    
2. Tip: While waiting for the URL to load, cameras can be partially set up and tablets can be fully set up.
    
3. After 25 minutes, test whether the hub provisioned successfully by attempting to access any of the following URLs:
    

- [http://heylo-hub.local:8123](http://heylo-hub.local:8123)
    
- [http://heylo-hub:8123](http://heylo-hub:8123)
    
- Also try ports 8080 and 8192, both with and without .local.
    
- (Refer to image below)
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

4. Once accessible, go to [http://heylo-hub.local:8123](http://heylo-hub.local:8123) and click "Create my smart home". Set the username, display name, and password according to the HA auth credentials. (Refer to image below)
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

5. Keep clicking "Next" until the "Finish" button appears, then click it.
    
6. If the UI is not as expected, return to Step 1 to re-flash the SD card. If the issue persists, message heylo-provisioning.
    

  
  

Steps:

Check downloads folder and remove any heylo.env file before starting 

Download the latest .wic file and extract it (it is usually compressed in bz2 format needs a 3rd party extraction tool on windows. (kept for viewing comment purposes)  
  

  

- If you’re on the workstation at Marconi. The path to the folder default value is C:\\Users\\Heylo\\Downloads.  Run the command with no path specified.
    

- inject-wic.bat "[WIFI SSID]" "[WIFI PASSWORD]”
    

(kept for viewing comment purposes)

---

# Adding Sensors and Cameras

This section outlines the process for pairing all peripheral devices with the central hub.

Device list will be provided by the team.

---

## Reolink Hub & Camera Setup

If using marconi workstation skip to step 1

Prerequisites: 

- Install reolink app from official website here:  https://reolink.com/software-and-manual/#reolink-app-client-download
    

  
  

|Component|Quantity|Notes|
|---|---|---|
||||
||||
||||
||||
||||

  
  
  
  

---

Adding a Reolink Hub

Open the Reolink kit and place the extras (mini screwdriver and SD card) into the Reolink Hub Extras Bin.

Plug the Reolink hub into ethernet and power.

1. The light should turn orange/green.
    

Open the Reolink Desktop App. 

Once the hub is plugged in and your computer is on the same network, the hub will appear in the app. (Refer to image below)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Get the Reolink username and password from the platform. (Refer to image below)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Set the password using the Reolink auth credentials from the Agency menu on our platform. (Refer to image below)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Set the device name to "[AgencyName SiteName] Reolink Hub", replacing the brackets with the actual agency and site name. (Refer to image below)

2. Note: The system is case sensitive. If the character limit is reached, prioritize the non-variable part of the name ("Reolink Hub"), as it is used for identification.
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Click "Finish".

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Click "Next" until the device has finished setting up.

  
  

Set password (kept for comment viewing purposes)

  

---

  

Adding a Reolink Camera (Wired/Wireless)

1. On a tablet or mobile device, open the Reolink app and tap "+" to add a camera.
    
2. Scan the QR code on the camera.
    
3. Choose the connection method:
    

- Wired cameras: Connect via Bluetooth.
    
- Doorbells/Wireless cameras: Connect via QR code.
    

5. If prompted about an SD card, tap "I got it".
    
6. Plug in and turn on the camera, then click "Next" twice.
    
7. Enter the site WiFi details.
    
8. Switch to the desktop Reolink app to complete setup.
    
9. If setting up with a hub:
    

- Click the arrow next to the Reolink hub to set it up with the hub.
    
- Choose "Add Device" within the hub menu and check the "Devices on this LAN" tab.
    

11. If setting up standalone (without a hub):
    

- Select the device from the Device list. Once it is on the same WiFi network as your computer, it will appear in the Reolink app device list with the model name labeled as "Not initialized".
    

13. Enter the Reolink password from the Agency menu in our platform using the "View Reolink Auth" button.
    
14. Name the device using the format: "[Common Area] [Wireless/Wired] Camera", replacing [Common Area] with the actual common area name.
    

- If the character limit is reached, prioritize the non-variable parts of the name (e.g., "Wireless Camera" or "Wired Camera"), as these are used for identification.
    
- Note: If setting up without a hub, include Agency and Site info in the name, following this format: "[Agency] [Site] [Common Area] [Wireless/Wired] Camera". Abbreviations may be used for Agency, Site, or Common Area if the name is too long, but always keep "Wireless Camera" or "Wired Camera" fully written out.
    

16. Select the device and open its settings:
    

- For wired cameras:
    

1. Click the gear icon for the device → Display → disable "Watermark". (Refer to image below)
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

2. Click "Stream" → set Frame Rate to 10 and Max Bitrate to 2048. Click save. (Refer to image below) 
    

3. Note: If 10 and 2048 are not options, prefer the lowest closest number.
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

3. Click “Smart Detection” -> “Sensitivity” -> Toggle the bottom bar to 0. (Refer to images below) 
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

4. Click “Light” -> turn off “Status LED”. (Refer to the image below)
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

5. Final Step for Wired Camera: Click “Sounds” -> make sure “Record Audio” is toggled on. (Refer to the image below)
    

![](data:image/png;base64,)

  

1. For wireless camera: 
    

  
  
  

2. For video doorbell: 
    

Notes: fix putty, reset the app on tablet to test camera on macaroni

  

Home Assistant Setup

1. Go to Home Assistant.
    
2. Navigate to Settings → Devices & Services and click the "Add Integration" button in the bottom right. Search for and select "Reolink".
    
3. Match the discovered Reolink device to the hub IP address found in the Reolink app:
    

- In the Reolink app, go to Hub Settings → Customize → Network → Network Information to find the IP address.
    
- Enter the username and password from the Reolink auth page in the Agency menu.
    
- Click "Skip", then "Finish".
    

5. Search for "ONVIF" in integrations.
    
6. Enable "Automatic search". If the hub does not appear, select "Manual entry".
    
7. On the manual entry screen, fill in the following fields:
    

- Name: AgencyName SiteName Reolink Hub ONVIF
    
- Host: Hub IP address
    
- Port: 8000
    
- Username/Password: Reolink auth credentials from the Agency menu
    

9. Click "Submit", then "Skip", then "Finish".
    

  

10. Additional settings 
    

11. For Video Doorbell: 
    

  

---

## Zigbee Device/Sensor Pairing

Repeat the following steps for each sensor:

1. Go to the Zigbee UI: [http://heylo-hub.local:8080/](http://heylo-hub.local:8080/)
    
2. Select "Permit to join" — this will stay active for 4 minutes. Select it as many times as needed to add all devices.
    
3. Remove and re-insert the battery in the sensor you are pairing to put it into pairing mode. It should be immediately picked up by the Zigbee UI if "Permit to join" is active.
    
4. Ensure all devices are stable and connected (there should be no spinning blue circle).
    
5. Click "Ping Hub" in the Hub menu on the Devices list in our platform to auto-add paired devices.
    
6. Refresh the page to see the newly added device.
    
7. Set the device destination and alert settings as needed.
    

---

If there is no Reolink Hub:

1. Open PuTTY on your computer. 
    

(Refer to image below)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

2. Select "Heylo-Hub", click "Load", then click "Open".
    
3. When prompted, enter "Heylo123" as the password.
    
4. Enter the command get-hub-id to retrieve the Physical Device ID.
    
5. Copy and paste the Physical Device ID into the Heylo website to add the hub.
    
6. Activate the hub.
    

## Z-wave Device Pairing (Smoke Detector)

To pair the smoke detector:

1.   
    

  

---

## Validation

1. Activate Hub to ping all devices  (kept for comment view purposes)
    

  

2. Activate the Hub to ping all devices.
    

3. Each device should appear in the UI.
    
4. Select each device and test using the monitor. Expected outcomes per device:
    

- Door Sensor: Displays "Closed" when the two sensors are touching; displays "Open" when pulled apart.
    
- Motion Sensor: Displays "False" when no motion is detected; displays "True" when motion is detected.
    
- Emergency Button: Displays "One" when pressed once; displays "Double" when pressed twice.
    

3. Deactivate the Hub after testing.
    

---

# Tablet Provisioning

This final section details the preparation and configuration of the tablet into kiosk mode with the Heylo App.

Connect the tablet to wifi during setup, don't copy data from online accounts or other devices. Just a normal manual setup with minimal features when given options.

Prerequisites:

- Site Wifi credentials from our platform
    
- ADB installed on the computer (Computer must have Android platform tools ([https://developer.android.com/tools/releases/platform-tools](https://developer.android.com/tools/releases/platform-tools)) installed, and ADB added to PATH to run ADB commands. Tutorial: [https://theflutterist.medium.com/setting-up-adb-path-on-windows-android-tips-5b5cdaa9084b](https://theflutterist.medium.com/setting-up-adb-path-on-windows-android-tips-5b5cdaa9084b))
    

Steps for Tablet Provisioning

1. Turn on the tablet.
    
2. Disable auto-blocker if/when prompted.
    
3. Do not set up a password for the device.
    
4. In the "For your review" tab, select the first and second options. Do not select the third optional option.
    
5. In the "Easy setup with another device" tab, click "Set up manually".
    
6. Connect the tablet to the site WiFi network. This may take a few minutes.
    
7. On the "Copy apps & data" tab, click "Don't copy".
    
8. Click “Skip” the Google sign-in option, then click "Skip" on the popup again.
    
9. Disable all options in the "Google Services" tab then click “Accept”.
    
10. In the "Protect your tablet" tab, click "Skip", then "Skip anyway".
    

11. Click "Forgot password or don't have an account".
    
12. Click "Forgot password".
    
13. Click "Skip sign-in".
    

14. In the "Samsung service legal information" tab, click "Skip".
    
15. In the "Samsung service permissions" tab, disable "Customization Service" and "Smart Suggestions", then click "Agree".
    
16. If asked for a birthdate, choose something 2000 or earlier.
    
17. In the "Check your region" tab, select "Other state" unless the site is in Utah, in which case select "Utah", then click "Next".
    
18. Choose "Light Display" and click "Next".
    
19. Click "Next".
    
20. Click "Finish".
    

---

Enable USB Debugging

1. Go to Settings → About tablet → Software information and tap "Build number" 7 times to enable developer mode.
    
2. Go to Settings → Developer options → enable "USB debugging" → click "OK".
    
3. Go to Settings → Security and privacy → turn off Auto Blocker if it is turned on. 
    

4. If there is no toggle button, then double tap Auto Blocker and turn it off.
    

5. Connect the tablet to your computer.
    

6. Tap "Allow" on the device data sharing popup.
    

Note: If there is an error or no popup, go to Developer options → tap "Revoke USB debugging authorizations" → tap "OK" → re-enable USB debugging → tap "Always allow" when prompted. The device should then appear on your computer.

---

Install the App and Enable Kiosk Mode

1. Make sure the tablet is connected to your computer.
    
2. Tap "Allow" on the USB debugging popup to authorize the computer.
    
3. Tap "Allow" on the file transfer popup.
    
4. Download the APK from Google Drive:
    

- [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UJT73gE7DrhuWHCH5QziUuBI9eVuqEy1/view?pli=1](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UJT73gE7DrhuWHCH5QziUuBI9eVuqEy1/view?pli=1)
    

6. Via CLI, run: adb install [PATH TO APK]
    

- Most likely found in your downloads folder.
    

8. Once installed, run the following commands from your computer's command prompt:
    

- adb shell settings put global protect_battery 1
    
- adb shell dpm set-device-owner com.heylo.app/.AppDeviceAdminReceiver
    

1. Note: If the terminal results in an exception error saying “Exception occurred while executing 'set-device-owner':” then in settings, select “Accounts and backup”, then “Manage accounts” then delete any accounts listed and rerun the command.
    

2. Note: If this is a development tablet (qa.heylo.tech instead of app.heylo.tech), use the following command instead: 
    

- adb shell dpm set-device-owner com.heylo.app.dev/com.heylo.app.AppDeviceAdminReceiver  
      
    

12. Disconnect the tablet from your PC. 
    
13. Open the Play Store and tap "Sign in". Sign in with:
    

- Email: tablet@heylo.tech
    
- Password: Smart1234!
    
- Click "I agree".
    

15. (If prompted) On the next tab, disable "Back up device data", then click "Accept".
    
16. (If prompted) Don’t allow local recommendations
    
17. Search for "Heylo Tech" in the Play Store and update the app.
    
18. Once updated, open recent apps (the three-bar button at the bottom of the screen) and swipe away all open apps.
    
19. Open the Heylo App.
    
20. Add the device in the Devices section of our platform, using the physical device ID displayed on screen as the device ID.
    
21. Wait a few seconds for the device to auto-register.
    
22. When prompted, grant permission to use the microphone and camera — select "While using app" for both.
    

---

Exiting and Re-entering Kiosk Mode

- To exit kiosk mode: While in kiosk mode, tap the top-right corner of the screen 5 times within 3 seconds to bring up the Exit Kiosk Mode dialog. Enter 2650 as the code (this may change).
    
- To re-enter kiosk mode: Open recent apps, swipe away the Heylo app, then re-launch it. It should automatically re-enter kiosk mode.
    

---

Validation

- Conduct test calls and view logs to confirm that call events are appearing correctly.
    

  

Work in progress: QR code automated Tablet provisioning

1. Generate QR code:
    

{

    "android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_DEVICE_ADMIN_COMPONENT_NAME": "com.heylo.app/.AppDeviceAdminReceiver",

    "android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_SKIP_EDUCATION_SCREENS": true,

    "android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_LEAVE_ALL_SYSTEM_APPS_ENABLED": true,

    "android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_WIFI_SSID": "YOUR_WIFI",

    "android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_WIFI_PASSWORD": "YOUR_PASSWORD",

    "android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_WIFI_SECURITY_TYPE": "WPA"

}

  
  

2. For testing purposes set wifi and pass to heylo-provisioning and heylo123
    
3. Use this link to generate QR code: [https://qr.munb.me/json-qr](https://qr.munb.me/json-qr)
    
4. After generating code, open tablet and tap the bottom right corner 6 times to set up tablet by scanning the QR code you just generated.
    
5. On the “This device belongs to your organization” tab, click next
    

  

## Final Checks

1. Verify that all sensors are reporting their status correctly 
    
2. Confirm that the live camera feeds are accessible and functional.
    
3. Exit kiosk mode before powering off when packing up to send to the customer.
    

  

|Task|Completion Date|Sign-off|
|---|---|---|
|Hardware Assembly Complete|Date|Person|
|Initialize Hub|Date|Person|
|Devices Paired|Date|Person|
|Devices Tested|Date|Person|
|Tablet Provisioning Complete|Date|Person|
|Tablet Calls Tested|Date|Person|
|Switch Marconi Wifi back|Date|Person|

  
  
  

Reviewed by:**
